title: Teaching activities
text:
Course materials repository
Work in progress… At the moment, all my current materials can be found on moodle.

title: Teaching activities
section: Teaching activities at Université Paris Cité
text:
Teaching activities at Université Paris Cité

Machine Learning I – Data Analysis
M1 – Master Ingénierie, Mathématique et Biostatistique (Université Paris Cité)
2024–2026 · Lectures, tutorials and practical sessions
Introduction to machine learning methods for high-dimensional data analysis.
Topics include supervised and unsupervised learning methods, model evaluation, and implementation using statistical software.


Statistics for Genetics and Genomics
M1 – Master Ingénierie, Mathématique et Biostatistique (Université Paris Cité)
2024–2026 · Lectures, tutorials and practical sessions
Course introducing statistical methods for the analysis of genetic and genomic data, including association studies, population structure and statistical modelling of genomic data.


EM Algorithm: a case study
M1 – Master Ingénierie, Mathématique et Biostatistique (Université Paris Cité)
2022–2026 · Lectures, tutorials and practical sessions
Applied course on the Expectation–Maximization algorithm, focusing on mixture models, latent variable models and practical implementation in statistical software.


Mathematics and Calculus I
L1 – Licence Mathématiques et Applications (Université Paris Cité)
2024–2026 · Tutorials
Tutorial sessions covering fundamental concepts in calculus and mathematical analysis for first-year undergraduate students.


Mathematics Refresher Course
L1 – Licence Mathématiques et Applications (Université Paris Cité)
2024–2026 · Tutorials
Support course aimed at strengthening mathematical foundations for undergraduate students entering the mathematics programme.

title: P-E Sugier
text: Since September 2024, I have been an Associate Professor at Université Paris Cité.

title: P-E Sugier
text:
Research Interests
My research is structured around two complementary directions: the development of statistical methods motivated by genetic and omics data analysis to investigate risk factors for complex diseases, and the application of advanced statistical methods to large-scale epidemiological data.


Machine-Learning
- High-dimension
- Meta-analysis models
- Bayesian models
- Variable selection

Quantitative Genomics
- Statistical genetics
- Epidemiology of complex diseases
- Multi-omics data integration

title: P-E Sugier
text:
Supervision and mentoring

PhD supervision

Christina Feng (since 2023), PhD student at the Department of Mathematics and Statistics, Macquarie University (Sydney, Australia). Joint supervision together with Benoit Liquet.
Thesis topic: Advancing multivariate methods for detecting pleiotropy using GWAS summary statistics.



Master’s supervision

Eva Jaber (2024), MSc internship in Health Data Science, Université Paris-Saclay.
Project: Methods for pleiotropy analysis with applications to Parkinson’s disease and cancers.
Donna-Lee Bowie (2022), MSc internship in Bioinformatics, Université de Bordeaux. Joint supervision with Yazdan Asgari. Project: Network enrichment analysis of pleiotropic genes for breast cancer and thyroid disorders.
Amélie Ngo (2021), MSc internship in Public Health Methods, Université Paris-Saclay.
Project: Analysis of shared genetic risk factors between Parkinson’s disease and cancers.

title: P-E Sugier
text:
Service and responsabilities

Since 2025 — Elected member of the Council of the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science, Université Paris Cité.

title: P-E Sugier
text:
Scientific Outreach

July 2023 — Invited by the International Parkinson and Movement Disorder Society (MDS) for a scientific interview in the official MDS podcast (Episode 120) discussing my research on pleiotropy between Parkinson’s disease and cancers.
“Pleiotropy in Parkinson’s disease and Cancer: Is there an association between these entities?”

title: Pierre-Emmanuel Sugier
text:
Since September 2024, I have been an Associate Professor at Université Paris Cité. I conduct my teaching within the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science and carry out my research at the MAP5 Laboratory (CNRS UMR 8145). I am also an associated researcher with the Exposome and Heredity team at the Center for Epidemiology and Population Health (CESP), Inserm.
Depuis septembre 2024, je suis maître de conférences à l’Université Paris Cité. J’effectue mes recherches au sein de l’UFR de Mathématiques et Informatique, et mes recherches au sein du laboratoire MAP5 (CNRS UMR 8145). Je suis également chercheur associé dans l’équipe Exposome et Hérédité du Centre de recherche en Epidémiologie et Santé des Populations (CESP) de l’Inserm.
